title: erinus alpinus subsp. hispanicus
intro: Erinus alpinus subsp. hispanicus, also known as the Iberian toadflax or Spanish toadflax, is a perennial plant species in the borage family, native to the Iberian Peninsula. It is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Erinus alpinus subsp. hispanicus include relieving coughs and respiratory issues, reducing inflammation and pain, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from its leaves and flowers, which can be consumed as a tea, infusion, or used as a topical application in the form of creams, salves, or ointments.
